ingredients.item: 2 cups (270 g) frozen peas (or a mix of peas and carrots)
ingredients.original: 2 cups (270 g) frozen peas (or a mix of peas and carrots)

ingredients.item: 2 cups (400 g) long-grain white basmati rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely
ingredients.original: 2 cups (400 g) long-grain white basmati rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely

ingredients.item:
FOR GARNISHING AND SERVING
1 tablespoon vegetable oil
½ cup (55 g) slivered almonds (optional)
ingredients.original:
FOR GARNISHING AND SERVING
1 tablespoon vegetable oil
½ cup (55 g) slivered almonds (optional)

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (13 g) fresh parsley leaves
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (13 g) fresh parsley leaves

ingredients.item: 1 In a large pot, heat the 3 tablespoons oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring often, until softened, about 5 minutes.
ingredients.original: 1 In a large pot, heat the 3 tablespoons oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring often, until softened, about 5 minutes.

ingredients.item: 2 Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up into small pieces with a wooden spoon, for 7 to 10 minutes, until browned and the juices start to dry up.
ingredients.original: 2 Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up into small pieces with a wooden spoon, for 7 to 10 minutes, until browned and the juices start to dry up.

ingredients.item: 3 Add the allspice, cinnamon, 1 teaspoon of the salt, the pepper, and peas. Stir to combine and continue to cook for 5 to 7 minutes, until the mixture looks dry and not much moisture is left.
ingredients.original: 3 Add the allspice, cinnamon, 1 teaspoon of the salt, the pepper, and peas. Stir to combine and continue to cook for 5 to 7 minutes, until the mixture looks dry and not much moisture is left.

ingredients.item: 4 Add the rice to the pot along with 3 cups (720 ml) of water and the remaining 1½ teaspoons salt. Mix well to combine and ensure the water completely covers the rice mixture. Increase the heat to medium-high and let the mixture come to a boil; this should take only a few minutes. As soon as it starts boiling, cover immediately with the lid and reduce the heat to low. Let cook for 20 minutes, undisturbed.
ingredients.original: 4 Add the rice to the pot along with 3 cups (720 ml) of water and the remaining 1½ teaspoons salt. Mix well to combine and ensure the water completely covers the rice mixture. Increase the heat to medium-high and let the mixture come to a boil; this should take only a few minutes. As soon as it starts boiling, cover immediately with the lid and reduce the heat to low. Let cook for 20 minutes, undisturbed.

ingredients.item: 5 MEANWHILE, IF USING THE ALMONDS AND/OR RAISINS, MAKE THE GARNISH: Heat the 1 tablespoon oil in a small skillet over medium-low heat, then add the almonds and toast them for 4 to 5 minutes, until golden brown, stirring continuously. Transfer to a small bowl. Add the raisins to the skillet and warm them through, about 30 seconds.
ingredients.original: 5 MEANWHILE, IF USING THE ALMONDS AND/OR RAISINS, MAKE THE GARNISH: Heat the 1 tablespoon oil in a small skillet over medium-low heat, then add the almonds and toast them for 4 to 5 minutes, until golden brown, stirring continuously. Transfer to a small bowl. Add the raisins to the skillet and warm them through, about 30 seconds.

ingredients.item: 6 After the rice has cooked for 20 minutes, turn off the heat and let the pot sit, covered, for 10 minutes more. Remove the lid and fluff the rice with a fork or wooden spoon,
ingredients.original: 6 After the rice has cooked for 20 minutes, turn off the heat and let the pot sit, covered, for 10 minutes more. Remove the lid and fluff the rice with a fork or wooden spoon,

ingredients.item: 7 Spoon into a serving dish and garnish with the almonds (if using), raisins (if using), and parsley. Serve with yogurt and salad.
ingredients.original: 7 Spoon into a serving dish and garnish with the almonds (if using), raisins (if using), and parsley. Serve with yogurt and salad.
